Ad Text:
BE THE DIFFERENCE
At AIA we don’t simply believe in being ‘the best’. We believe in better – because there’s no limit to how far ‘better’ can take us. Everything we do is driven by our purpose to make a difference by helping people embrace Healthier, Longer, Better Lives. And every one of our people has an important role to play. If you want to make a difference by helping shape a healthier, better-protected Australia, read on.

Your next opportunity:

We are sourcing a Reporting and Insights Manager for a 12-month fixed term contract, to join our team at AIA in either Melbourne or Sydney.

Within this role you will be responsible for liaising with business stakeholders to create requirements, design, develop, enhance and implement reporting and insight solutions for AIA Health. You will help drive planning, estimation and varied tasks associated with both strategic and operational business objectives.

To do this successfully you will:

Partner with relevant Operations teams, Distribution, Data Analytics, Retention, and Product teams to solve problems and identify trends and opportunities

Distil large volumes of data, information and/or complex concepts in a clear and concise manner for key stakeholders

Validate business demands and document high level functional requirements to ensure the new/modified functionalities address the business requirement

Leverage industry and sector experience to bring progressive best practice solutions to AIA Health

Managing requirements and briefs from the business, and size the work appropriately to coordinate sprint planning

Build Business Intelligence (BI) solutions using Tableau Software & Power BI

Provide technical expertise in areas of architecture, design, and implementation

Design, develop, conduct unit testing, and maintain Tableau/Power BI dashboards, as well as schedule extract refreshes

Design dashboard wireframes with an emphasis on user experience, consistency, and visibility

Successful applications for this role will demonstrate:

Tertiary qualification in Technology or related discipline

Solid domain knowledge within the insurance or financial services industry

Strong analytic Tools capabilities (SQL, R) a significant advantage.

Strong capability with dashboard tools (i.e.) Tableau, Microsoft Power BI

Familiarity with Tableau Administrator/Architecture

Knowledge of and experience in reporting systems, data platforms, relational database management systems and data modelling

The ability to communicate succinctly and persuasively, both verbally and through written presentations, and influence a broad range of stakeholders

A delivery focused mindset and exceptional customer service

Strong attention to detail and time management skills

Why choose AIA:

At AIA, we’ve made a promise to help people live healthier, longer, better lives. And it starts with our own people.

Access our training and development to build on your current skills

Career development through internal mobility opportunities

Work for a business helping millions of Australian’s and make a difference to someone’s life everyday

Access additional leave days a year to recharge and refresh yourself

Enjoy wonderful Health and Wellbeing initiatives that support you

Work with supportive and inclusive managers

Flexible working arrangements

At AIA Australia, we’re proud to help guide, support and protect the wellbeing of over 3.5 million Australians. Through life and health insurance, financial wellbeing and our total wellbeing ecosystem that includes AIA Vitality – the world’s leading science-backed wellbeing program – we’ve started a movement to make Australia the healthiest, best protected nation in the world.

As part of the AIA Group - the largest independent publicly listed pan-Asian life insurance group with a presence in 18 markets in Asia-Pacific – it is our ambition to engage one billion people to live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives by 2030.  Lots of companies like to say they’re different. What makes us truly different at AIA, is the difference we make in people’s lives.

AIA is different. Are you?

If you want to be part of an organisation that helps people live better every single day, apply via the link prior to Friday 22nd May 2026.

AI Business Analyst

Translate Business Needs for AI Projects  

Sydney / Contract / Hybrid

Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Here's what happens on every enterprise AI engagement without a good BA: the Solutions Architect designs a technically elegant integration. The engineers build it beautifully. And three months later, the client says "this isn't what we needed"

The gap between what a business actually needs and what a technical team builds is where projects go to die. AI projects are especially vulnerable because the technology is so capable that it's easy to build impressive things that don't solve the right problems.

Someone needs to sit between the business stakeholders who know what they need (but can't articulate it technically) and the delivery team who can build anything (but don't know what's most valuable).

That someone is you.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. AI Business Analysts are the translators — the people who ensure every Claude integration we build solves a real business problem that was properly understood, scoped, and prioritised.

What you'll do on client engagements
Run discovery workshops with business stakeholders to identify high-value AI use cases — not "where could we use AI?" but "where are you losing time, money, or quality that AI could directly address?"
Document business requirements and translate them into technical specifications that Solutions Architects and Integration Engineers can execute against
Map existing business processes end-to-end, then identify where Claude integration creates the most value — eliminating manual steps, improving decision quality, accelerating throughput
Build business cases for AI use cases — quantifying time savings, error reduction, and productivity gains so clients can prioritise investments
Conduct AI readiness assessments — evaluating whether a team's processes, data, and culture are ready for Claude integration, and identifying gaps to address first
Define acceptance criteria and success metrics for each integration — so there's a clear, agreed definition of "done" and "working"
Manage requirements throughout the engagement — tracking changes, resolving conflicts between stakeholder groups, and ensuring scope stays aligned with value
What you bring
4+ years in business analysis, management consulting, process improvement, or similar analytical roles
Strong requirements gathering skills — you can facilitate a workshop with many stakeholders who all want different things and leave with a clear, prioritised requirements document
Process mapping and analysis experience — you can document how work actually gets done (not how the org chart says it should) and identify improvement opportunities
Business case development — you can quantify benefits, estimate costs, and build the financial justification for investment decisions
Stakeholder management — you navigate conflicting priorities, manage expectations, and build consensus across business and technology teams
Clear written and verbal communication — your requirements documents are unambiguous, your presentations are compelling, and your workshop facilitation is engaging
Bonus (not required)
CBAP, PMI-PBA, or equivalent business analysis certification
Experience with AI, automation, or digital transformation projects
Consulting background — particularly in the Big 4, specialist consulting, or enterprise advisory
Industry experience in financial services, government, healthcare, or professional services
Familiarity with Agile methodologies — user stories, acceptance criteria, backlog management
AI is creating more demand for business analysts, not less. The companies deploying Claude need people who can figure out what to build before they build it — and that's a fundamentally human skill. The BAs who develop AI domain expertise will be the most sought-after analysts in the market, because they combine traditional BA rigour with genuine understanding of what AI can and can't do.

Apply today for a confidential discussion

This role sits within the projects finance function of a large organisation delivering major infrastructure programs across Australia. The position exists to strengthen financial oversight across a significant capital portfolio and acting as a key link between teams.

The role partners closely with project commercial teams, finance business partners and delivery stakeholders to consolidate, review and challenge project financial performance. This position focuses on forecasting, capex reporting and executive‑level financial insight to ensure projects remain commercially and financially on track.
  

Key Responsibilities:

Partner with project commercial teams and finance stakeholders across major infrastructure projects
Consolidate and review project forecasts, budgets and cost‑to‑complete reporting
Prepare monthly project performance packs, capex reporting and executive summaries
Deliver variance analysis and provide clear commentary on project risks, cost movements and delivery impacts
Support project governance, investment reporting and financial oversight across the capital portfolio
Work closely with Group Finance to ensure accurate portfolio reporting and forecast integrity
Assist with cash flow forecasting, financial modelling and scenario analysis
Challenge assumptions and provide commercial insight to support decision‑making

Key Skills and Experience We’re Looking For:

CA or CPA qualified
Experience in FP&A, project finance, commercial finance or finance business partnering
Exposure to infrastructure, construction, engineering, utilities or large‑scale project environments highly regarded
Strong forecasting and reporting capability
Ability to partner effectively with operational and non‑finance stakeholders
Strong communication skills, with the ability to present financial information clearly to senior stakeholders
